title: RAPA-EX-01 Trial: Weekly Rapamycin May Blunt Exercise Gains in Older Adults
description: The RAPA-EX-01 randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial, published April 25 in the Journal of Cachexia, Sarcopenia and Muscle, found that once-weekly sirolimus (rapamycin) 6 mg did not enhance exercise adaptations in sedentary adults aged 65-85 and may have modestly attenuated functional improvements from a 13-week home exercise program. Sensitivity analyses reached statistical significance favoring placebo (per-protocol: -3.44 repetitions, p=0.007), and the rapamycin group had a higher adverse event burden including one serious case of pneumonia. This complicates the popular clinical practice of combining rapamycin with exercise protocols and raises important questions about dosing timing and interactions that future trials must resolve.

title: Senescent p21+TREM2+ Liver Macrophages Identified as Central Drivers of Inflammaging
description: A landmark study published April 16 in Nature Aging identified a distinct p21+TREM2+ senescent macrophage population as a major source of chronic inflammation in aging and metabolic liver disease (MASLD), with macrophages representing 60-80% of all senescent p21+ cells in the aged liver. Senolytic treatment targeting this population with ABT-263 reduced liver inflammation and steatosis in both aged mice and MASLD models, cutting senescent macrophage prevalence from ~50% back to ~10%. This finding reshapes our understanding of inflammaging by pinpointing liver-resident Kupffer cells as a tractable therapeutic target and providing a precise molecular signature (p21+TREM2+) for senescent macrophage identification.

title: BioAge BGE-102 NLRP3 Inhibitor Shows Best-in-Class Inflammation Reduction in Phase 1
description: BioAge Labs reported Phase 1 data for BGE-102, a brain-penetrant oral NLRP3 inhibitor, showing median hsCRP reductions of 85% at day 7 and 86% at day 21 in the 60 mg once-daily cohort, with 87% of participants normalizing hsCRP to below the 2 mg/L cardiovascular risk threshold. The drug was well tolerated across all dose levels with no serious adverse events or discontinuations, and also reduced IL-6 and fibrinogen. These results are particularly significant for longevity medicine because NLRP3-driven inflammaging is a central hallmark of biological aging, and a brain-penetrant compound capable of normalizing systemic inflammation at this magnitude represents a potential geroprotective platform.

title: FTC/TAF HIV Drug Demonstrates First Human Proof-of-Concept for Retrotransposon-Targeted Gerotherapeutics
description: A medRxiv preprint from University of Colorado and UC San Diego researchers showed that 12 weeks of emtricitabine-tenofovir alafenamide (FTC/TAF, Descovy) reduced PhenoAge by 6.33 years and slowed DunedinPACE in healthy HIV-negative adults, representing the first human proof-of-concept for retrotransposon-targeted gerotherapeutics. The mechanism involves TAF's superior intracellular concentration in immune cells, suppressing LINE-1 retrotransposon activity that fuels cGAS-STING-driven inflammaging, with concordant reductions in epigenetic IL-6 and brain aging clocks. The comparator drug FTC/TDF showed no such effects, establishing formulation specificity and opening an entirely new drug class for aging intervention using already-approved, well-tolerated medications.

title: Interferon-Pathway Inflammaging Causally Drives Epigenetic Age Acceleration and Multimorbidity
description: A Cell Genomics paper published April 17 used Mendelian randomization to establish that interferon-pathway cytokines, especially CXCL9, are causal drivers of epigenetic age acceleration across multiple clocks (GrimAge, PhenoAge, EAA) and are linked to multimorbidity, not merely correlated with it. The findings argue the interferon/IFN pathway is a priority anti-aging intervention target, with CXCL9 showing the strongest causal link to faster epigenetic aging and increased mortality risk via GrimAge. Paradoxically, cells that were epigenetically older performed worse against actual pathogens despite elevated inflammatory tone, illustrating the fundamental dysfunction of inflammaging.

title: RAPA-EX-01: Weekly Rapamycin Attenuates Exercise Benefits in Sedentary Older Adults
source: Journal of Cachexia, Sarcopenia and Muscle
description: A 13-week randomized, double-blind trial (RAPA-EX-01) of weekly 6 mg sirolimus vs. placebo in 40 sedentary adults aged 65-85 found no enhancement of exercise-induced functional gains, with sensitivity analyses significantly favoring placebo on chair-stand performance and a higher adverse event burden (99 vs. 63 events) in the rapamycin arm, including one serious pneumonia.

title: p21+TREM2+ Senescent Macrophages Fuel Inflammaging and Liver MASLD
description: UCLA researchers identified p21+TREM2+ senescent macrophages as the dominant source of inflammaging in the aging liver, rising from 5% in young mice to 50-80% in aged animals, with senolytic ABT-263 treatment restoring levels to ~10% and reducing liver steatosis and inflammation in both aged and MASLD mouse models.

title: BioAge Reports Phase 1 Data for BGE-102, a Novel Brain-Penetrant NLRP3 Inhibitor
description: BioAge's oral NLRP3 inhibitor BGE-102 produced median hsCRP reductions of up to 86% in a multiple ascending dose Phase 1 trial, with 87-93% of treated participants normalizing hsCRP below 2 mg/L, alongside reductions in IL-6 and fibrinogen, all with no serious adverse events or discontinuations.

title: Interferon-Related Inflammaging Links Epigenetic Age Acceleration to Multimorbidity
description: Mendelian randomization analysis published in Cell Genomics identified CXCL9, CXCL10, CCL11, and IL-18 from the interferon pathway as causal drivers of epigenetic age acceleration across GrimAge, PhenoAge, and EAA clocks, with age-related inflammaging paradoxically impairing immune cell pathogen response despite elevated baseline inflammation.

title: FDA-Approved HIV Drug FTC/TAF Reduces Biological Age by ~6.3 Years in Healthy Adults
description: A University of Colorado/UC San Diego preprint demonstrated that 12 weeks of emtricitabine-tenofovir alafenamide (FTC/TAF) reduced PhenoAge by 6.33 years (p=0.008) and slowed DunedinPACE (p=0.019) in 36 healthy HIV-negative adults, representing the first human proof-of-concept that retrotransposon suppression via an FDA-approved NRTI can produce measurable gerotherapeutic effects.

title: BCLXL-PROTAC Senolytic Clears Senescent Cells and Promotes Lung Regeneration in COPD
description: Published in Aging Cell, a BCLXL-PROTAC proteolysis-targeting chimera selectively induced apoptosis in senescent small airway epithelial cells and fibroblasts from COPD patients, reducing p21CIP1, p16INK4a, and SASP markers while increasing Ki67 proliferation marker, validated in precision-cut COPD lung tissue slices without affecting non-COPD cells.

title: AlzeCure Completes Phase Ib Study of ACD856 NeuroRestore Cognitive Candidate
description: AlzeCure Pharma announced the last patient's last visit in the Phase Ib trial of ACD856, a Trk-PAM modulator of BDNF and NGF signaling designed to restore neuronal communication for cognitive decline and depression. The compound previously demonstrated blood-brain barrier penetration at relevant concentrations, preclinical memory improvement, and EU Innovation Council grant support, with Phase II preparation now underway.

title: AB Science Masitinib Yields 121-Month Median Survival in ALS Long-Term Survivors
description: A medRxiv preprint from AB Science's Phase 2b/3 AB10015 study showed masitinib 4.5 mg/kg/day produced a 5-year survival rate of 42.3% versus ~23.5% historical controls, with a median overall survival of 121 months in long-term survivors versus 42 months predicted by the ENCALS model, supporting a prospective confirmatory trial (AB23005) in 408 patients.

title: Ghrelin Receptor Inhibition Improves Muscle Function and Mitophagy in Aging Mice
description: Deletion or pharmacological inhibition (PF-5190457) of GHSR-1a, the ghrelin receptor, improved muscle fatigue resistance, endurance, and grip strength in aged mice (24-28 months) without affecting longevity or muscle mass, with improvements linked to enhanced mitochondrial biogenesis (PGC-1alpha) and mitophagy (PINK1/p62), identifying GHSR-1a as a novel therapeutic target for sarcopenia.

title: Homoharringtonine (HHT) Identified as Novel Senotherapeutic That Extends Mouse Lifespan
description: Screening 2,150 FDA-approved compounds, researchers identified HHT, an approved CML treatment, as a potent senolytic targeting HSPA5-expressing senescent adipocytes, reducing adipose tissue fibrosis and metabolic dysfunction in obese and aged mice while extending lifespan in both progeroid and naturally aged mice, with confirmed activity in human white adipose tissue ex vivo.

title: FDA-Approved Terbinafine and Miglustat Extend Lifespan via Mitohormesis in C. elegans
description: A screen of 770 FDA-approved drugs identified antifungal terbinafine and glucosylceramide synthase inhibitor miglustat as mitohormetic compounds that activate UPRmt and DAF-16/IIS signaling, extending C. elegans lifespan by 7-9% (terbinafine) and 5.5% (miglustat) while reducing age-related paralysis by up to 60%, with confirmed mitochondrial stress induction in human cells.

title: Tozorakimab Meets Phase III Primary Endpoint in COPD MIRANDA Trial
description: AstraZeneca's IL-33-targeting monoclonal antibody tozorakimab achieved a statistically significant and clinically meaningful reduction in moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations across former smokers (primary population) and the overall trial population, including across all blood eosinophil counts and disease severity stages, adding to earlier OBERON and TITANIA Phase III wins.

title: Mesoblast Licenses CAR Technology from Mayo Clinic to Enhance MSC Therapies
description: Mesoblast acquired an exclusive worldwide license to a chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) platform from Mayo Clinic to enhance its mesenchymal lineage stromal cells for more precise inflamed-tissue targeting in conditions including inflammatory bowel disease and lupus nephritis, building on its FDA-approved Ryoncil therapy while Mayo Clinic continues GMP manufacturing support.

title: FGF8 Gene Therapy Partially Restores Digit Regeneration in Mice Using Salamander Pathway
description: Researchers identified conserved SP6/SP8 transcription factors as key regulators of limb regeneration across axolotl, zebrafish, and mice, then used an AAV-delivered zebrafish enhancer-FGF8 gene therapy to partially rescue digit tip regeneration in SP knockout mice (32% greater bone volume at 28 days) and accelerate regeneration in wild-type mice by 34%, demonstrating a contextual gene therapy path toward human limb regeneration.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences Phase 1 RLS-1496 Data Show Senolytic Efficacy in Human Skin
description: Rubedo Life Sciences reported Phase 1 results for RLS-1496, a topical GPX4 modulator that met its primary safety endpoint across plaque psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and photo-aged skin, while showing dose-dependent senescent cell reduction, a 20% average decrease in epidermal thickness in psoriasis at one month, and increased collagen gene and protein expression in photo-aged skin.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Major Clinical Trial of Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin for Aging
description: UT Health San Antonio launched a NIA-funded multi-phase clinical study testing rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin as potential aging interventions in approximately 700 older South Texan adults, evaluating biological markers, optimal dosing, and long-term effects, representing one of the largest human aging drug trials ever initiated.

title: Long-Term Acoramidis Treatment Yields 45% Mortality Reduction Through 54 Months in ATTR-CM
description: The ATTRibute-CM open-label extension showed continuous acoramidis treatment through 54 months produced a 45% reduction in all-cause mortality, 49% reduction in cardiovascular mortality, and 47% reduction in first CV hospitalization versus the placebo-to-acoramidis switch group, with sustained NT-proBNP stabilization and no new safety concerns.

title: Hone Health Integrates DEXA Scans into Physician-Led Longevity Platform
description: Hone Health partnered with BodySpec to integrate clinical-grade DEXA scans into its telehealth longevity platform, enabling physicians to receive automated body composition data including visceral fat, bone density, and resting metabolic rate, creating a continuous feedback loop between diagnostics and personalized longevity treatment adjustments.

title: Life Time Rolls Out SpiroFit VO2 Max and Metabolic Testing Nationally
description: Life Time announced a national rollout of SpiroFit metabolic testing technology across its North American athletic country clubs, providing lab-grade VO2 max measurement within 1-3% accuracy via a wearable cordless mask, translating results into five-zone training profiles to make elite-level cardiorespiratory fitness testing accessible for longevity optimization.

title: ITP Results: Astaxanthin, Pioglitazone, and 9 Other Compounds Fail to Extend Mouse Lifespan
description: The NIA Interventions Testing Program rigorously tested 11 compounds in UM-HET3 mice across three independent sites, finding none extended lifespan; notably, astaxanthin and pioglitazone reduced female lifespan, underscoring the critical importance of multi-site validation and sex-specific endpoints before recommending supplements or repurposed drugs for human longevity use.

title: PEPITEM Replacement Therapy Shows Anti-Inflammatory Potential Exceeding Infliximab in Arthritis Models
description: A synthetic form of PEPITEM, an anti-inflammatory peptide that declines with aging, prevented arthritis and gout in mouse models with greater swelling reduction than infliximab, reduced inflammation and cartilage damage, and restored immune regulation in human PBMCs, offering a novel approach to age-related inflammatory joint disease by restoring a naturally declining immunoregulatory peptide.

title: Sex-Specific Differences in Aging Interventions: Mouse Evidence and Translational Implications
description: A comprehensive review in Ageing Research Reviews catalogued how dietary, genetic, environmental, and pharmacological longevity interventions in mice show substantial sexual dimorphism, with X-chromosome linked factors, gonadal hormones, and ovarian biology being critically understudied contributors, calling for sex-stratified trial designs in translational aging research.

title: Air Pollution Accelerates Biological Aging and Dementia Risk via Epigenetic Mechanisms
description: A UK Biobank prospective cohort study linked long-term exposure to PM2.5 (HR 1.141), NO2 (HR 1.20), and NOx (HR 1.14) to increased dementia risk, brain volume reduction, and accelerated biological aging via KDM-BA and PhenoAge clocks, with air pollution shown to advance onset of 48 out of 78 chronic conditions by 2-5 years on average.

title: Preclinical Senolytics Clear Up to 70% of Zombie Cells; Senosensitizers May Address Resistant Remainder
description: A Cedars-Sinai preclinical study published in Aging Cell showed senolytic drugs eliminate 30-70% of senescent cells while leaving less-harmful senolytic-resistant cells behind; infections can re-activate these residual cells into tissue-damaging states, and a new class of 'senosensitizers' may enable senolytics to clear these resistant cells, with potential applications in post-chemotherapy residual cancer senescence.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment on Track for $8-9 Billion Full-Year Record in 2026
description: Longevity.Technology data shows Q1 2026 longevity biotech financing reached $3.74 billion across 49 events, a 56% increase over Q1 2025, with annualized projections of $8-9 billion for full-year 2026 likely to surpass 2021 as the record year. Capital is concentrating into later-stage platforms in cellular reprogramming, epigenetic medicine, and cardiometabolic longevity, with median deal size rising to $21.8 million.
